mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which drives corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 or Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production signal weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day equity returns in over 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 210.0 indicates accelerating price pressures that force markets to anticipate tighter financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 210.0, or the threshold loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending capacity and supports corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day equity returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.03 or a negative alpha in a multivariate regression over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining retail sales signals a demand shock that compresses profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2.0% and RSAFS month-over-month change < 0.5%
failure_condition: The joint slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields positive 20-day returns in over 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Improvements in the index of industrial production during post-shock recovery phases signal restored manufacturing capacity that lifts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 50% or a negative information coefficient over any 18-month rolling window.
